archetypes.label: Passive investor
archetypes.summary: Buy-and-hold index and ETF investor. No alpha-seeking. Long horizon.

archetypes.label: Property investor
archetypes.summary: Owns one or more investment properties. May be negatively geared. Rentvesters fall here.

archetypes.label: Active investor
archetypes.summary: Returns depend on own decisions or structure. Includes FIRE, frequent traders, and trust-led strategies.

archetypes.label: Founder / business owner
archetypes.summary: Material equity in a private operating business. Exit gain dominates lifetime wealth.

lifeStages.label: Early-career accumulator (20s)
lifeStages.summary: Building first portfolio; little CGT history; may rent; pre-FHB.

lifeStages.label: Mid-career builder (30s)
lifeStages.summary: Often first-home buyer or recent buyer; growing portfolio; possibly young family.

lifeStages.label: Peak-earner (40s)
lifeStages.summary: Top-bracket income common; portfolio compounding; super contributions material.

lifeStages.label: Pre-retiree, bridge phase (50s)
lifeStages.summary: Building non-super assets to bridge to preservation age; CGT timing matters.

lifeStages.label: Retiree, decumulation (60+)
lifeStages.summary: Drawing down; pension-phase super possible; lower marginal-rate windows matter.

scenarios.label: Passes as announced
scenarios.summary: Discount replaced by indexation plus a 30 percent floor from 1 Jul 2027; NG restricted to new builds.

scenarios.label: Delayed past 2027 election
scenarios.summary: Start date pushed back or paused; status quo survives through the election cycle.

scenarios.label: Repealed or not legislated
scenarios.summary: Package fails or is wound back. Current CGT discount and negative gearing persist.

scenarios.label: Founder relief carve-out added
scenarios.summary: Package passes with founder-specific relief for qualifying private-business gains.

scenarios.label: 30 percent floor dropped, indexation kept
scenarios.summary: Minimum tax removed; inflation-indexed cost base remains.

scenarios.label: Indexation dropped, discount plus floor kept
scenarios.summary: No indexation, but the discount survives alongside a minimum effective rate.

demographic.label: Pensioner
demographic.summary: Income-support recipient, including age pension or DSP.

economic.label: Owner-occupier
economic.summary: Owns primary residence and reads policy through household cash flow.

economic.label: Landlord
economic.summary: Holds investment property and cares about deduction treatment.

economic.label: Asset-rich retiree
economic.summary: Material non-super assets and drawdown exposure.

electoral.label: Inner-metro progressive
electoral.summary: Inner-city Labor or Greens seats with large professional class exposure.

electoral.label: Outer-suburb mortgage belt
electoral.summary: Marginal outer-suburb seats shaped by mortgage stress and household cash flow.

electoral.label: Regional
electoral.summary: Non-metropolitan seats with different business and housing exposures.

electoral.label: Teal seat
electoral.summary: Independent-held former Liberal seats with high professional investor exposure.

electoral.label: Safe Coalition
electoral.summary: Safe Liberal or Nationals seats where landlord and small-business narratives travel well.

cells.action: Stage pre-2027 parcel sales only where they serve a real near-term goal, and keep fresh savings on the long-horizon plan rather than abandoning equities.
cells.actionRationale: Passive portfolios mostly win by delaying unnecessary tax and avoiding panic turnover. Early-career readers still have time on their side, so the main hazard is overreacting to policy noise with costly churn. Under the announced branch, pre-2027 and post-2027 treatment diverge sharply, so timing and valuation-reset mechanics matter.
cells.payoffNarrative: Main value is avoiding premature complexity while preserving optionality. Upside comes from respecting the regime split rather than pretending every gain is taxed the same way.

regret.description: If the package is repealed, the move crystallised tax or reshaped the balance sheet earlier than necessary. The main cost is lost deferral, transaction friction, and opportunity cost. For passive investors, the regret usually shows up as unnecessary realisation or needless churn.

cells.keyAssumption: The portfolio horizon is real and the investor is not about to force a sale for liquidity reasons that the action ignores. Early mistakes are mostly behavioural, so overreaction is the dominant risk. The announced mechanics survive broadly intact through legislation and implementation.
cells.cohortNote: This cell is most politically salient where long-horizon household savers read tax reform through portfolio simplicity, deposit timing, and retirement-bridge concerns.

cells.action: Avoid irreversible pre-emptive sales and keep building under the current rules while preserving records for a possible later transition.

The surviving discount and floor combination is legislated in a form close enough to this scenario to plan against.
cells.cohortNote: Salience is concentrated where startup density, professional capital, and innovation politics overlap, so the cell travels hardest in inner-metro and teal discussions even when the direct taxpayer base is small.
